title: OpenArm shared 3A primitives (CTR-PRIM@v1)
description: The single definition point of the six primitives the five 3A schemas (CTR-CAM/CAP/TEL/WS/REC) share. Frozen by content hash (WP-3A-00); a byte change is a CI-09 freeze violation. This is the language-agnostic mirror of contracts/prim/schema.py; the two must not diverge (02b §5.0b).

camera_identifier.description: One slot-key grammar shared as the CAM registry key, the CAP sidecar column stem, the WS binary frame tag, and the REC observation.images.<slot> key.

timestamp_domain.description: CLOCK_MONOTONIC nanoseconds. The server clock is the sole expiry judge; the client clock is an age input only. The synthetic dataset grid timestamp is orthogonal to a real capture timestamp.

frame_type.description: Image channel kinds. RGB is a required capability; depth is optional. One enum for CAM capability, WS channel tag and REC feature key.

action_payload.description: Position-only action, unit-tagged in degrees, re-exported from CTR-ACT@v1. Never carries velocity or torque dimensions.

queue_semantics.description: Every real-time queue is bounded, priority-classed, and carries both a drop policy and a drop classification (normal/defect/counted) so a dropped frame means one thing across every quality report.

error_envelope.description: One wrapper around a registered CTR-ERR@v1 code: {code, reason, severity}. Surfaced identically by all five contracts.
